title: 10 best herbal tinctures for jet lag
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for jet lag are concentrated liquid extracts of plants used to alleviate symptoms associated with travel across time zones. They help regulate sleep patterns, reduce fatigue, and promote relaxation, making it easier to adjust to new time zones. The best herbal tinctures for jet lag include Valeriana officinalis, Passiflora incarnata, Melissa officinalis, Ginkgo biloba, Lavandula angustifolia, Avena sativa, Zingiber officinale, Panax ginseng, Schisandra chinensis, and Withania somnifera. Without them, jet lag can severely impact daily life, leading to irritability, decreased productivity, and even physical health problems, ultimately affecting work, relationships, and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with jet lag because its sedative properties promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, making it easier to adjust to a new sleep schedule. As valerian interacts with GABA receptors in the brain, it can help regulate the body's internal clock, allowing travelers to fall asleep faster and sleep more soundly. Additionally, valerian tinctures can increase the quality of sleep, reducing grogginess and fatigue associated with jet lag. This natural remedy helps alleviate the discomfort of traveling across time zones, making it easier to feel rested and refreshed upon arrival at your destination.
remedy_properties: Sedative properties: Valerian root has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help regulate sleep patterns and reduce stress caused by jet lag.
Adaptogenic properties: Valerian's adaptogenic properties allow it to help the body adjust to changes in environment and routine, making it easier to adapt to new time zones and rhythms.
Anxiolytic properties: Valerian has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can reduce anxiety and restlessness caused by jet lag, promoting relaxation and improved sleep.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Acts as a GABA receptor agonist, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety that often accompany jet lag.
Valeranone: Exhibits sedative and anxiolytic properties, helping to regulate sleep patterns and alleviate symptoms of jet lag.
Isovaleric acid amide: Displays sedative and hypnotic effects, contributing to improved sleep quality and reduced sleep latency, which is often disrupted by jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain a high concentration of valerenic acid, a key compound responsible for the sedative and relaxing properties of Valerian root.
Buds: They are rich in valerenic acid and other bioactive compounds that help to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety associated with jet lag.
Stems: They contain a higher percentage of valerenic acid compared to leaves, making them a preferred choice for tincture production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots and leaves in the early morning or late afternoon for optimal potency.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material to prevent contamination and spoilage for 7 to 10 days.
Combine the dried plant material with 60 to 70 percent ethanol in a glass jar to create a 1:3 ratio.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and allow the mixture to macerate for 2 to 3 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with jet lag because its calming and sedative properties can help regulate your body's internal clock. The herb contains rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to improve sleep quality by reducing stress and anxiety. By promoting relaxation and calming the mind and body, lemon balm tincture can help you adjust to a new time zone faster and get a good night's sleep, even when traveling across multiple time zones. This can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag, such as fatigue, insomnia, and disorientation.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Lemon balm's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by long-distance travel, promoting a smoother transition to local time zones.

Calming and Sedative Effects: The calming and sedative properties of lemon balm tincture can help regulate sleep patterns and promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep at the new destination's bedtime.

Digestive Soothing Properties: Lemon balm's carminative and anti-spasmodic compounds can alleviate digestive discomforts associated with air travel, such as bloating, cramps, and nausea, allowing for a more comfortable journey.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound helps alleviate jet lag by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress caused by disrupted circadian rhythms.
Volatile oils (especially linalool and linalyl acetate): These terpenes have a sedative effect, promoting relaxation and regulating sleep patterns, which is essential for adapting to new time zones.

Melissic acid: This triterpenoid compound has been shown to have a regulatory effect on the body's internal clock, helping to reset the circadian rhythms and alleviate symptoms associated with jet lag, such as insomnia and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melissa officinalis are used to make tinctures for jet lag because they contain a high concentration of the bioactive compound melissic acid, which helps regulate sleep patterns.
Flowers: The flowers of Melissa officinalis are used to make tinctures for jet lag because they have been traditionally used to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation.

Roots: The roots of Melissa officinalis are used to make tinctures for jet lag because they contain a rich source of melatonin, which helps regulate the body's internal clock and improve sleep quality.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa leaves and flowers at peak potency in mid-summer morning after the dew has dried.
Dry the melissa leaves and flowers at 30 degrees celsius for 24 hours in a well-ventilated area.
Combine 250 grams of dried melissa leaves and flowers with 750 milliliters of 35% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily for 5 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discard the solids and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tinctures helps with jet lag because of its unique properties that regulate the body's internal clock. The tincture contains a compound called passiflora incarnata, which has been shown to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels, making it easier for the body to adjust to new time zones. Additionally, maypop's adaptogenic properties help the body adapt to changes in its natural rhythms, reducing symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, insomnia, and disorientation.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic effects: Maypop tincture helps your body adapt to new environments and rhythms, reducing stress and anxiety caused by travel-induced disruptions.

Sedative and calming properties: The flavonoids and alkaloids in Maypop tincture promote relaxation and calmness, making it easier to fall asleep despite the body's natural resistance to sleep after crossing time zones.
Antioxidant activity: Maypop tincture's antioxidant properties help mitigate oxidative stress caused by jet lag, which can lead to fatigue, irritability, and other symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Hernandezioside: This flavonoid glycoside has sedative properties, which can help regulate the body's internal clock and alleviate symptoms associated with jet lag.
Isovitexin: This flavonoid has been found to have a sedative effect, potentially helping to reduce the disruption of the body's natural sleep-wake cycle caused by long-distance travel.
Isoorientin: This flavonoid has been reported to have anxiolytic and sedative properties, which can help reduce stress and anxiety often experienced during jet lag.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and alkaloids, which have been shown to have a sedative effect and help regulate sleep patterns.
Fruits (or more specifically, the pulp): The pulp of the fruit is rich in flavonoids and may help alleviate symptoms of jet lag by promoting relaxation and regulating sleep.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of fresh or dried passiflora incarnata leaves and flowers and clean them thoroughly.
Combine the cleaned passiflora incarnata with 500ml of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks or 2 to 3 months.
Strain the liquid from the jar through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container.
Filter the tincture again through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with jet lag because they contain a unique blend of adaptogenic compounds that help regulate the body's natural circadian rhythms. The tincture's gentle, balancing effects on the nervous system and hormonal fluctuations can alleviate symptoms of fatigue, insomnia, and disorientation commonly experienced during time changes. Additionally, maidenhair tree's calming properties can reduce stress and anxiety associated with travel, allowing for a smoother transition back to local time. This natural remedy can help you adjust to your new environment and get a good night's sleep in no time.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals in the body, which can contribute to fatigue and disorientation caused by jet lag.

Cerebral vasodilator effects: Ginkgo biloba's ability to dilate blood vessels in the brain can help improve circulation and reduce inflammation, which may alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as headaches and cognitive fogginess.

Adaptogenic properties: Ginkgo biloba is known for its adaptogenic effects, meaning it helps the body adapt to stress and change, which can be particularly helpful when traveling across time zones and adjusting to a new environment.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress caused by jet lag, promoting better sleep and reducing the severity of fatigue.
Bilobalide: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms such as headaches and eye strain associated with jet lag.
Quercetin: This type of flavonoid has been shown to regulate circadian rhythms and improve sleep quality, making it easier to adjust to new time zones.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are commonly used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to help alleviate jet lag symptoms.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are used for their ability to promote antioxidant activity and improve circulation, potentially helping to mitigate the effects of jet lag.
Roots: The roots of Ginkgo biloba are sometimes used for their purported adaptogenic properties, which may help the body adjust to changes in time zones and reduce fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves in late summer or early fall when they are fully ripe and dry.
Clean the leaves thoroughly to remove any dirt or debris with distilled water.
Combine 1 cup of the cleaned ginkgo biloba leaves with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats tinctures helps with jet lag because they contain soothing compounds like avenanthramides, which can calm the nervous system and promote relaxation. This allows your body to adjust to its new time zone more easily, reducing symptoms of fatigue, insomnia, and disorientation that often accompany jet lag. Additionally, oat tinctures have been shown to regulate cortisol levels, helping to stabilize circadian rhythms and facilitate a smoother transition into local time. By promoting deep relaxation and regulating bodily functions, oat tinctures can help mitigate the effects of jet lag and get you feeling refreshed and revitalized in no time.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Properties: Oat tincture helps the body adapt to new time zones by regulating cortisol levels and promoting a sense of balance, reducing stress and anxiety caused by jet lag.
Sedative and Relaxing Effects: The tincture's calming properties can help induce relaxation and reduce insomnia, making it easier to fall asleep at a new bedtime and adjust to the local time zone.

Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Activity: Oat tincture's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties can help mitigate the negative effects of jet lag on the body, such as fatigue, headaches, and digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Silicic acid: Helps with jet lag by promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, which can alleviate symptoms of fatigue and jet lag-related stress.
Avenanthramides: May help with jet lag by regulating the body's circadian rhythms and promoting a sense of balance and calm, which can aid in readjusting to a new time zone.
Saponins: Contributes to the herb's potential benefits for jet lag by exhibiting adaptogenic properties, which can help the body adapt to new environments and time changes.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of Avena sativa are used to make tinctures to help with jet lag due to their high concentration of tryptophan, an amino acid that promotes relaxation and sleep.
Roots: The roots of Avena sativa are used to make tinctures for jet lag because they contain a compound called avenacoside, which helps regulate the body's internal clock and reduce jet lag symptoms.

Stems: The stems of Avena sativa are used to make tinctures for jet lag as they are rich in flavonoids and other compounds that have a sedative effect and help alleviate symptoms of jet lag, such as insomnia and fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest avena sativa roots and leaves in bulk quantity, usually 1 kilogram for tincture making.
Chop the harvested roots and leaves into small pieces, about 1/4 inch in size, for easier extraction.
Combine the chopped avena sativa material with 80% vodka and 20% water in a 1:3 ratio by weight.
Steep the mixture in a dark glass container for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid from the solid material through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with jet lag because it calms the mind and body, reducing symptoms of fatigue, insomnia, and anxiety often associated with time zone changes. The tranquil properties of lavender tincture work to regulate sleep patterns and promote a restful night's sleep, allowing travelers to adjust quickly to their new environment. Additionally, lavender's adaptogenic effects help to normalize the body's natural rhythms, ensuring a smoother transition to the local time zone.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic properties: Lavender's anxiolytic effects can help reduce stress and anxiety caused by travel disruptions, allowing for a smoother transition to a new time zone.
Sedative properties: The sedative compounds in lavender tincture can promote relaxation and improve sleep quality, helping to counteract the effects of jet lag on your body's internal clock.

Adaptogenic properties: Lavender's adaptogenic effects enable it to help regulate the body's response to stress and adapt to new situations, which is particularly useful when traveling across time zones and experiencing disruptions in daily routine.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene helps with jet lag by promoting relaxation, reducing stress, and regulating the body's natural sleep-wake cycle, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone.

Linalyl acetate: This ester has a calming effect on the body and mind, which helps alleviate the symptoms of jet lag, such as insomnia and restlessness, allowing the body to recover from travel fatigue.

Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the oxidative stress and inflammation caused by traveling across time zones, promoting a smoother recovery from jet lag.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to make tinctures for jet lag because of their calming and relaxing properties, which help alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as insomnia and anxiety.
Leaves: They are used to make tinctures for jet lag because they contain compounds that have a balancing effect on the body's circadian rhythms, helping to regulate sleep patterns.
Stems: They are used to make tinctures for jet lag because they contain bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, which can aid in the recovery from jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and 2 parts of vodka or glycerin as solvent.
Combine the dried flowers and solvent in a clean glass jar for 2-6 weeks to steep.
Shake the jar daily for 2-6 weeks to facilitate extraction of herbal properties.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Store the herbal tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St john's wort tinctures helps with jet lag because of its unique ability to regulate the body's internal clock. As a natural adaptogen, it helps the body adjust to new time zones by promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing stress levels. By calming the mind and body, St john's wort tincture allows individuals to easily transition into their new surroundings, minimizing the effects of jet lag and ensuring a smoother travel experience.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: St. John's Wort helps the body adapt to stress, including the physical and mental fatigue caused by jet lag, allowing you to adjust more easily to new time zones.

Melatonin regulation: St. John's Wort has been shown to regulate melatonin levels in the body, which can help alleviate symptoms of insomnia, anxiety, and other sleep disturbances commonly experienced during jet lag.

Mood-enhancing effects: St. John's Wort is known for its antidepressant properties, which can help reduce feelings of irritability, anxiety, and stress that often accompany jet lag, promoting a more relaxed and balanced state.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: This terpene helps alleviate jet lag by modulating the body's circadian rhythms and regulating sleep-wake cycles.

Flavonoids (specifically, rutin and hyperoside): These phenolic compounds contribute to the antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of St. John's Wort, which may aid in reducing fatigue and promoting a healthy sleep-wake balance.

Hypericin: This naphthodianthron compound has been shown to have a regulating effect on the body's internal clock, potentially helping to mitigate the effects of jet lag and improve overall sleep quality.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain flavonoids and glycosides, which are believed to help regulate the body's internal clock and reduce symptoms of jet lag.
Flowers: Rich in hyperforin and hypericin, which have been shown to have adaptogenic properties that can aid in adjusting to new time zones.
Roots: They are a good source of hyperforin and other bioactive compounds that can help regulate the body's circadian rhythms and reduce fatigue associated with jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-30% of the st. john's wort plant's above-ground parts when flowers are in full bloom.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer for 1-2 weeks in a well-ventilated area.
Combine 1 part of the dried st. john's wort with 2 parts of a 95% ethanol solution in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2-4 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn tinctures helps with jet lag because of its adaptogenic properties, which allow it to gently adjust the body's natural rhythms. When taken at the right times, hawthorn tincture can help regulate sleep patterns and ease the symptoms of jet lag, such as fatigue, insomnia, and restlessness. Its calming effects also promote relaxation and reduce stress levels, making it easier to fall asleep on a new schedule. By supporting the body's natural circadian rhythms, hawthorn tincture helps to minimize the effects of jet lag and get you feeling refreshed and ready for your trip.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Hawthorn helps your body adapt to changes in time zones by reducing stress and promoting relaxation, which can alleviate symptoms of jet lag like fatigue and insomnia.

Cardiovascular benefits: Hawthorn's ability to improve blood flow and reduce blood pressure can help alleviate symptoms of dehydration and fatigue that often accompany jet lag, while also promoting overall cardiovascular health.

Antioxidant properties: Hawthorn's antioxidants help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by travel and time changes, which can contribute to inflammation and fatigue; they also support liver function and promote healthy detoxification processes.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds, particularly quercetin and epicatechin, are known to regulate circadian rhythms and have antioxidant properties, which can help the body adapt to new time zones and reduce fatigue associated with jet lag.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids like chlorogenic acid and caffeic acid in Crataegus monogyna may help to reduce inflammation and improve sleep quality, which can be beneficial in managing jet lag.

Triterpenes and saponins: These compounds may have a regulating effect on the body's internal clock and have been shown to reduce stress and anxiety, which can be useful in coping with the physical and mental challenges of jet lag.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used to make tinctures due to their adaptogenic properties, which help the body adapt to changing time zones and reduce stress.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make tinctures for their antioxidant properties, which help protect the body from the negative effects of jet lag, such as fatigue and oxidative stress.
Fruits: The fruits are used to make tinctures due to their adaptogenic and antioxidant properties, which help regulate the body's circadian rhythms and reduce symptoms of jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Gather 50g of dried hawthorn berries from crataegus monogyna and clean them thoroughly.
Combine 50g of dried hawthorn berries with 500ml of 45% ethanol in a clean glass container.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the container occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Transfer the tincture into dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place indefinitely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with jet lag because its calming properties can soothe a restless body and mind, allowing you to adjust to new time zones more easily. The herb's natural sedative effects can promote relaxation and reduce stress levels, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep during your destination's local bedtime. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate symptoms of fatigue and irritability that often accompany jet lag.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) properties: Chamomile tincture helps to calm nervousness and anxiety associated with jet lag, making it easier to fall asleep and relax.
Sedative effects: Chamomile's sedative properties help to reduce restlessness, irritability, and insomnia caused by disrupted sleep patterns due to jet lag.

Anti-inflammatory and soothing effects: Chamomile tincture can also help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation in the digestive system, which can alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as stomach upset and bloating.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: An apigenin is a flavonoid that has been shown to promote relaxation and reduce stress, which can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue and disorientation.
Beta-pinene: Beta-pinene is a terpene that can help regulate sleep patterns and improve the quality of sleep, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone when traveling.
Chamazulene: Chamazulene is a sesquiterpene lactone that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate fatigue and discomfort associated with jet lag.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Matricaria chamomilla are commonly used to make tinctures due to their high apigenin content, which is known to help with relaxation and sleep.
Seeds: The seeds of Matricaria chamomilla are also used to make tinctures, likely due to their potential to aid in relaxation and sleep, although less information is available on this specific use.

Roots: The roots of Matricaria chamomilla are sometimes used to make tinctures, possibly due to their potential to promote relaxation and reduce inflammation, although more research is needed to confirm this use.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 to 2 pounds of fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers at dawn when they are fully open and dry.
Chop the flowers into small pieces to release the oils and prepare them for extraction.
Combine 1 pound of the chopped flowers with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain and filter the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Figwort tinctures helps with jet lag because it contains a unique blend of herbs that work in harmony to regulate the body's natural rhythms. The figwort plant has been used for centuries to treat various health issues, including fatigue and insomnia, which are common symptoms of jet lag. The tincture helps to calm the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. Additionally, it can help to regulate the body's internal clock, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Figwort's adaptogenic properties allow it to help your body adjust to new time zones by reducing stress and fatigue, making it easier to acclimate to a new sleep-wake cycle.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in figwort tincture can help neutralize free radicals caused by jet lag-induced oxidative stress, which can contribute to symptoms like fatigue, headaches, and digestive issues.

Stress-relieving and calming effects: Figwort's sedative and anxiolytic properties can promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep despite the disruption of your regular sleep schedule.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the figwort plant contain a high concentration of alkaloids, which help to regulate the body's internal clock and reduce symptoms of jet lag.
Barks: The barks of the figwort plant have been traditionally used to treat circadian rhythm disorders, making them effective for alleviating jet lag symptoms such as insomnia and fatigue.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds have antioxidant properties, which may help mitigate the oxidative stress caused by jet lag and subsequent sleep disturbances.
Scrophularia alkaloids: These alkaloids have been reported to have sedative and sleep-promoting effects, which could help alleviate insomnia and other sleep disorders associated with jet lag.
Iridoids: These compounds have been found to have adaptogenic properties, which may help regulate the body's circadian rhythms and improve the body's ability to cope with the stress of jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of fresh or dried roots and leaves of scrophularia nodosa to make tincture.
Combine gathered scrophularia nodosa with 2 parts of 80 proof ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks to allow maceration.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Transfer the resulting tincture into dark glass bottles for storage and use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava tinctures helps with jet lag because they promote relaxation, reduce anxiety, and regulate sleep patterns. The calming effects of kava can help alleviate the stress and fatigue that often accompanies long-distance travel across time zones. By supporting a healthy circadian rhythm, kava tinctures can aid in adjusting to new sleep schedules, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep. This natural remedy can be especially helpful for individuals who struggle with insomnia or restlessness when traveling abroad.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) effects: Kava's anxiolytic properties help reduce stress and anxiety caused by travel disruptions, making it easier to adjust to new time zones.

Muscle relaxant and sedative effects: Kava's muscle relaxant and sedative properties can help alleviate insomnia, restlessness, and fatigue often experienced during jet lag, promoting a good night's sleep.

Adaptogenic and calming effects: Kava's adaptogenic properties allow it to adjust to an individual's unique response to stress, helping to stabilize mood and reduce irritability that can come with crossing time zones.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are effective in promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety associated with jet lag.
Rhyzomes: As the main storage organ of the plant, rhyzomes contain high levels of kavalactones, which help regulate sleep patterns and reduce stress caused by time zone changes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh kava roots in the morning after the first rain of spring or fall weighing 100-150 grams.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm dark place for 7-10 days monitoring moisture levels daily.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and add 70% ethanol to create a maceration mixture at 1:5 ratio.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks shaking the container daily allowing the tincture to extract active compounds slowly.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth and a coffee filter discarding solids then store in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asiatic pennywort tinctures helps with jet lag because they contain adaptogenic properties that help regulate the body's natural rhythms. The herb has been traditionally used to calm the mind and reduce stress, which is often exacerbated by travel disruptions. By promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, asiatic pennywort tincture can help ease the transition back into a normal sleep-wake cycle after crossing time zones. Its ability to adapt to the body's needs also makes it an effective natural remedy for jet lag, allowing individuals to recover quickly and feel more refreshed.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Asiatic Pennywort helps your body adapt to new environments and rhythms, reducing stress and fatigue associated with travel.
Antioxidant properties: This herb's antioxidant compounds help protect cells from oxidative damage caused by jet lag-induced inflammation and stress.
Calming and relaxing effects: Asiatic Pennywort's sedative and anxiolytic compounds promote relaxation, ease anxiety and insomnia, and help regulate sleep-wake cycles disrupted by travel.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots help to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety associated with jet lag.
Rhyzomes: Containing a high concentration of antioxidants, rhyzomes aid in reducing inflammation and improving circulation, which can alleviate symptoms of jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh centella asiatica leaves and stems from a trusted source weighing approximately 200 grams.
Rinse the plant material gently with filtered water to remove dirt and debris then pat dry with paper towels.
Combine the cleaned centella asiatica with 1000 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar for maceration.
Allow the mixture to steep for 14 days at room temperature away from direct sunlight with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi tinctures helps with jet lag because of its ability to calm the mind and body, promoting relaxation and reducing stress. The herb's adaptogenic properties allow it to help regulate the body's natural circadian rhythms, making it easier to adjust to new time zones. By supporting brain function and improving memory, brahmi tinctures can also help reduce fatigue and improve overall sleep quality, which is essential for recovering from jet lag. Regular use of brahmi tinctures can minimize the discomforts associated with traveling across time zones.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic and Sedative Properties: Brahmi's anxiolytic and sedative properties can help calm the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep despite time zone changes.

Adaptogenic Properties: Brahmi's adaptogenic properties allow it to help the body adapt to stress and change, including the physical and mental effects of jet lag, by promoting balance and regulating cortisol levels.
Cognitive Enhancing Properties: Brahmi's cognitive enhancing properties can help improve concentration and mental clarity, making it easier to navigate travel fatigue and adjust to new schedules.
remedy_parts: Roots: Brahmi roots are a popular choice for making medicinal tinctures as they contain a high concentration of adaptogenic compounds, which help regulate the body's response to stress and fatigue caused by jet lag.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Brahmi plant contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to improve sleep quality, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal tinctures for jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried bacopa monnieri herb measuring one part by weight for every five parts of vodka or ethanol.
Combine the dried herb and liquid in a clean glass jar leaving about one inch of headspace for expansion.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place allowing it to steep for at least two weeks shaking occasionally.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Label and store the tincture in a cool dark place for up to one year taking 10-20 drops as needed before bedtime.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha tinctures helps with jet lag because it promotes relaxation and reduces stress levels, which are often exacerbated by travel. The herb's adaptogenic properties help to regulate cortisol levels and improve sleep quality, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone. Additionally, ashwagandha has been shown to increase melatonin production, helping to regulate the body's natural rhythms and reduce symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, insomnia, and irritability. By using an herbal ashwagandha tincture, travelers can help their bodies adapt more quickly to new schedules and arrive at their destination feeling refreshed and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Ashwagandha helps to regulate the body's stress response, allowing it to adapt to changes in sleep-wake cycles and reduce symptoms of fatigue and insomnia associated with jet lag.

Antioxidant activity: Ashwagandha's antioxidant properties help to mitigate oxidative stress caused by travel-related inflammation, which can exacerbate symptoms of jet lag, such as fatigue and mood disturbances.

GABA-enhancing properties: Ashwagandha has been shown to increase the production of GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that promotes relaxation and helps regulate sleep patterns, making it easier to adjust to new time zones.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in adaptogenic compounds, ashwagandha roots help regulate circadian rhythms and reduce stress related to time zone changes.
Barks: The bark contains withanolides, which have been shown to alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as insomnia, fatigue, and anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried withania somnifera roots weighing approximately one kilogram.
Grind the dried roots into a coarse powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix one part of the powdered root with two parts of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for 14 days to allow for extraction, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: New Jersey tea tinctures helps with jet lag because of its calming and balancing properties, which can help regulate the body's internal clock. The tincture contains herbs like lemon balm, chamomile, and lavender, known for their sedative effects, which can promote relaxation and reduce stress caused by travel fatigue. By soothing the nervous system and promoting a sense of calm, New Jersey tea tinctures can ease the symptoms of jet lag, such as insomnia, anxiety, and fatigue, allowing travelers to adjust to new time zones more smoothly.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: New Jersey tea tincture helps the body adapt to stress, including the stress of travel and time zone changes, allowing for a smoother transition.
Calmative and sedative effects: The tincture's calming properties can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep despite the time difference.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: New Jersey tea contains antioxidants that can help mitigate the negative effects of jet lag on the body, such as fatigue, headaches, and digestive issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the New Jersey tea plant have been traditionally used to help regulate sleep-wake cycles and reduce symptoms of fatigue associated with jet lag.
Barks: The bark of the plant is rich in antioxidants and has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate stress and promote relaxation during times of travel.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried ceanothus americanus leaves and stems in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Chop 100g of plant material into small pieces and combine with 400ml of vodka in a glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks to allow maceration and extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding solids.
Transfer the tincture to amber glass dropper bottles filled to the top and store in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops tinctures helps with jet lag because of its adaptogenic properties that help regulate the body's internal clock. When taken before bedtime, the tincture's calming effects can help signal to the brain that it's time to sleep, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep despite a changed environment. Additionally, hops' natural sedative properties can reduce stress and anxiety often associated with jet lag, allowing for a smoother transition to a new time zone.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anxiety-relieving) properties: Hops have been shown to possess anxiolytic compounds like luteolin and xanthohumol, which can help reduce stress and anxiety caused by travel disruptions.
Sedative and hypnotic effects: The tincture's sedative properties can help regulate sleep patterns and promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep despite the disruption of a new time zone.

Adaptogenic properties: Hops have been found to have adaptogenic effects on the body, which means it can help your body adjust to changes in environment, stress, and rhythm, potentially reducing symptoms of jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in soothing compounds, hop roots help to calm anxiety and promote relaxation, making it an effective treatment for jet lag-induced stress.
Buds: Hop buds are rich in humulone, a compound that has been shown to regulate the body's internal clock and improve sleep quality.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh humulus lupulus hops and dry them in a warm, dark place for at least seven days.
Grind one part dried humulus lupulus into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered humulus lupulus with 40% ethanol vodka in a clean glass jar at a ratio of one part herb to three parts solvent.
Store the mixture in a cool, dark place for six weeks, shaking the jar every other day to ensure proper extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and labeling the tincture with preparation date and dosage instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena tinctures helps with jet lag because of its adaptogenic properties, which allow it to gently regulate the body's natural rhythms. The herb's soothing essence calms the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that often accompanies travel disruptions. remedy_properties: Anxiolytic and Sedative Properties: Lemon verbena tincture's ability to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety helps alleviate symptoms of jet lag, such as restlessness and insomnia.

Adaptogenic Properties: Lemon verbena's adaptogenic properties allow it to help the body adjust to changes in environment, routine, and stress caused by travel, reducing fatigue and promoting relaxation.
Hormone Regulating Properties: Lemon verbena tincture has been shown to regulate cortisol levels, which can become disrupted due to jet lag, helping to promote a sense of balance and normalcy.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots have been traditionally used to promote relaxation and reduce stress, making them ideal for combating jet lag-induced fatigue.
Barks: The barks of lemon verbena contain flavonoids that help regulate the body's circadian rhythms, which is essential for adapting to new time zones and minimizing the effects of jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried verbena officinalis leaves and flowers at peak potency for maximum extraction.
Weigh 10% of the total weight in 80-proof vodka or glycerin as the menstruum for 4-6 weeks.
Combine verbena officinalis material with menstruum in a clean glass jar, ensuring all parts are fully submerged.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking daily to facilitate extraction and maceration.
Strain and filter the resulting tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle for storage and use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lime tinctures helps with jet lag because they contain a blend of herbs that work synergistically to regulate the body's internal clock. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh flowers of tilia x europaea in the early morning.
Cleanly dry the harvested flowers using paper bags or a food dehydrator on low heat.
Crush dried flowers into small pieces and mix with 60% vodka or brandy in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 14 days at room temperature in the dark, shaking daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into a clean dark glass bottle, discard solids.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jet lag that are treated with herbal tinctures are those caused by time zone changes, fatigue, and disruptions to the body's natural rhythms. These natural remedies can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag and other circadian rhythm disorders.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with jet lag is a blend of ashwagandha, rhodiola, and valerian root. Valerian root promotes relaxation and improves sleep quality, which is crucial for recovering from jet lag. Taking 10-20 drops of each tincture, combined with a balanced diet and regular exercise, can help alleviate symptoms and adjust to the new time zone more efficiently.
